sms_messages.body:
Hey Michael - Erina just reached out to me about Sunday's sermon and asked me to reach out to you. I'm preaching on Leviticus 25 and the year of jubilee which is clearly tied to land ownership and with everything that's going on in Israel and Palestine and she thought it'd be good for me just to get a pulse check from you on where the congregation is. 

My sermon doesn't touch a ton on it in part because I made a clear distinction between ancient Israel and the present day secular state of Israel. I didn't want people to get the two things intermingled. I do want to address the current situation at the beginning of my sermon though- any guidance or thoughts from you would be appreciated. Specifically, if there's something you want to make sure I do NOT say, please let me know. Happy to jump on a call if that's easier.

Hi Sally, thanks for reaching out. I trust how you want to handle things as they relate to your sermon. We are a congregation that deals a lot in decolonizing our faith, and I think folks are able to recognize and long for the end of all violence, historical and present.
Also, we will be singing a song from Palestine, so there's that 🙂
I figured that would be the general tone at your church, but good to confirm. I look forward to hearing the song 💜
